Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A traffic light controller that cycles through Red, Green, Yellow states.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE TrafficLight ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES state, timer

(* State can be "Red", "Green", or "Yellow" *)
State == {"Red", "Green", "Yellow"}

(* Timer counts down from a preset value for each state *)
Timer == 0 .. 100

(* Type invariant *)
TypeOK == state \in State /\ timer \in Timer

(* Initial state: Red with timer at 60 *)
Init == /\ state = "Red" /\ timer = 60

(* Next-state relation *)
Next ==
  \/ /\ state = "Red" /\ timer > 0
     /\ state' = "Red" /\ timer' = timer - 1
  \/ /\ state = "Red" /\ timer = 0
     /\ state' = "Green" /\ timer' = 30
  \/ /\ state = "Green" /\ timer > 0
     /\ state' = "Green" /\ timer' = timer - 1
  \/ /\ state = "Green" /\ timer = 0
     /\ state' = "Yellow" /\ timer' = 5
  \/ /\ state = "Yellow" /\ timer > 0
     /\ state' = "Yellow" /\ timer' = timer - 1
  \/ /\ state = "Yellow" /\ timer = 0
     /\ state' = "Red" /\ timer' = 60

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, timer>>

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A token ring protocol where N nodes pass a token in a circle. Only the node holding the token may enter the critical section.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE TokenRing ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N

VARIABLES token, inCS

Init == /\ token = 1
        /\ inCS = {}

Next == \/ /\ token' = %(token, N) + 1
          /\ inCS' = inCS
        \/ /\ token' = token
          /\ inCS' = inCS \cup {token}

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<token, inCS>>

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

Peterson's algorithm for mutual exclusion between two processes.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE Peterson ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES turn, flag, state

Init == /\ turn = 0
        /\ flag = [i \in 1..2 |-> FALSE]
        /\ state = [i \in 1..2 |-> "idle"]

Next == \/ /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![1] = TRUE]
          /\ turn' = 2
          /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![1] = "trying"]
        \/ /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![2] = TRUE]
          /\ turn' = 1
          /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![2] = "trying"]
        \/ /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![1] = FALSE]
          /\ turn' = turn
          /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![1] = "critical"]
        \/ /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![2] = FALSE]
          /\ turn' = turn
          /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![2] = "critical"]
        \/ /\ flag' = flag
          /\ turn' = turn
          /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![1] = "idle"]
        \/ /\ flag' = flag
          /\ turn' = turn
          /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![2] = "idle"]

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<turn, flag, state>>

A state machine for a vending machine that accepts coins and dispenses items when enough money is inserted.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE VendingMachine ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES balance, inventory, selectedItem

Init == /\ balance = 0
        /\ inventory = [item \in {"Soda", "Chips", "Candy"} |-> 5]
        /\ selectedItem = ""

Next == \/ /\ balance = 0
          /\ selectedItem = ""
          /\ inventory = [item \in {"Soda", "Chips", "Candy"} |-> 5]
          /\ balance' = 0
          /\ inventory' = inventory
          /\ selectedItem' = selectedItem
        \/ /\ balance > 0
          /\ selectedItem # ""
          /\ inventory[selectedItem] > 0
          /\ balance >= 2
          /\ balance' = balance - 2
          /\ inventory' = [inventory EXCEPT ![selectedItem] = inventory[selectedItem] - 1]
          /\ selectedItem' = ""
        \/ /\ balance > 2
          /\ selectedItem # ""
          /\ inventory[selectedItem] > 0
          /\ balance >= 3
          /\ balance' = balance - 3
          /\ inventory' = [inventory EXCEPT ![selectedItem] = inventory[selectedItem] - 1]
          /\ selectedItem' = ""

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<balance, inventory, selectedItem>>

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

An elevator controller for a building with N floors. The elevator moves up and down, opening doors at requested floors.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE Elevator ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N

VARIABLES currentFloor, direction, requests

Init ==
    /\ currentFloor = 1
    /\ direction = 1
    /\ requests = {}

Next ==
    \/ /\ currentFloor \in 1..N
       /\ direction = 1
       /\ currentFloor < N
       /\ requests = {}
       /\ currentFloor' = currentFloor + 1
       /\ direction' = direction
       /\ requests' = requests
    \/ /\ currentFloor \in 1..N
       /\ direction = 1
       /\ currentFloor < N
       /\ requests # {}
       /\ currentFloor' = currentFloor
       /\ direction' = direction
       /\ requests' = requests \ {currentFloor}
    \/ /\ currentFloor \in 1..N
       /\ direction = 1
       /\ currentFloor = N
       /\ requests = {}
       /\ currentFloor' = currentFloor
       /\ direction' = -1
       /\ requests' = requests
    \/ /\ currentFloor \in 1..N
       /\ direction = 1
       /\ currentFloor = N
       /\ requests # {}
       /\ currentFloor' = currentFloor
       /\ direction' = direction
       /\ requests' = requests \ {currentFloor}
    \/ /\ currentFloor \in 1..N
       /\ direction = -1
       /\ currentFloor > 1
       /\ requests = {}
       /\ currentFloor' = currentFloor - 1
       /\ direction' = direction
       /\ requests' = requests
    \/ /\ currentFloor \in 1..N
       /\ direction = -1
       /\ currentFloor > 1
       /\ requests # {}
       /\ currentFloor' = currentFloor
       /\ direction' = direction
       /\ requests' = requests \ {currentFloor}
    \/ /\ currentFloor \in 1..N
       /\ direction = -1
       /\ currentFloor = 1
       /\ requests = {}
       /\ currentFloor' = currentFloor
       /\ direction' = 1
       /\ requests' = requests
    \/ /\ currentFloor \in 1..N
       /\ direction = -1
       /\ currentFloor = 1
       /\ requests # {}
       /\ currentFloor' = currentFloor
       /\ direction' = direction
       /\ requests' = requests \ {currentFloor}

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<currentFloor, direction, requests>>

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

The dining philosophers problem with N philosophers and N forks. Each philosopher picks up both adjacent forks to eat, then puts them down.

Hints: Track fork ownership and philosopher states: thinking, hungry, eating.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE DiningPhilosophers ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N

VARIABLES forks, states

(* Forks: array of length N, each element is 0 (free) or philosopher index (1..N) holding it *)
(* States: array of length N, each element is "thinking", "hungry", or "eating" *)

(* Helper functions *)
Left(i) == IF i = 1 THEN N ELSE i - 1
Right(i) == IF i = N THEN 1 ELSE i + 1

(* Type invariant *)
TypeOK == 
    /\ forks \in [1..N -> 0..N]
    /\ states \in [1..N -> {"thinking", "hungry", "eating"}]

(* Initial state: all forks free, all philosophers thinking *)
Init == 
    /\ forks = [i \in 1..N |-> 0]
    /\ states = [i \in 1..N |-> "thinking"]

(* Action: philosopher i becomes hungry *)
Hungry(i) == 
    /\ states[i] = "thinking"
    /\ states' = [states EXCEPT ![i] = "hungry"]
    /\ forks' = forks

(* Action: philosopher i picks up forks if both are free *)
PickUp(i) == 
    /\ states[i] = "hungry"
    /\ forks[Left(i)] = 0
    /\ forks[Right(i)] = 0
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![Left(i)] = i, ![Right(i)] = i]
    /\ states' = [states EXCEPT ![i] = "eating"]

(* Action: philosopher i puts down forks *)
PutDown(i) == 
    /\ states[i] = "eating"
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![Left(i)] = 0, ![Right(i)] = 0]
    /\ states' = [states EXCEPT ![i] = "thinking"]

(* Next-state relation: any philosopher can become hungry, pick up forks, or put down forks *)
Next == 
    \E i \in 1..N :
        (Hungry(i) \/ PickUp(i) \/ PutDown(i))

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<forks, states>>

---- MODULE DiningPhilosophers ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N, Null

VARIABLES forks, state

(* Forks: forks[f] = p means fork f is held by philosopher p; forks[f] = Null means fork f is free *)
(* State: state[p] = "thinking" | "hungry" | "eating" *)

Init ==
  /\ forks = [f \in 1..N |-> Null]
  /\ state = [p \in 1..N |-> "thinking"]

LeftFork(p) == IF p = 1 THEN N ELSE p - 1
RightFork(p) == IF p = N THEN 1 ELSE p + 1

CanEat(p) ==
  forks[LeftFork(p)] = Null /\ forks[RightFork(p)] = Null

Hungry(p) == state[p] = "hungry"

Eat(p) ==
  forks[LeftFork(p)] = p /\ forks[RightFork(p)] = p

Next ==
  \/ \E p \in 1..N :
       /\ state[p] = "thinking"
       /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![p] = "hungry"]
       /\ forks' = forks
  \/ \E p \in 1..N :
       /\ CanEat(p)
       /\ state[p] = "hungry"
       /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![p] = "eating"]
       /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT
                     ![LeftFork(p)] = p,
                     ![RightFork(p)] = p]
  \/ \E p \in 1..N :
       /\ Eat(p)
       /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![p] = "thinking"]
       /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT
                     ![LeftFork(p)] = Null,
                     ![RightFork(p)] = Null]

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<forks, state>>

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

The dining philosophers problem with N philosophers and N forks. Each philosopher picks up both adjacent forks to eat, then puts them down.

Hints: Track fork ownership and philosopher states: thinking, hungry, eating.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE DiningPhilosophers ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N

VARIABLES forks, states

(* Forks: a mapping from fork indices to philosopher indices or 0 if free *)
Forks == [f \in 1..N |-> IF f \in 1..N THEN 0 ELSE 0]

(* Philosopher states: 0=thinking, 1=hungry, 2=eating *)
States == [p \in 1..N |-> 0]

Init == forks = Forks /\ states = States

(* Helper functions *)
Left(p) == IF p = 1 THEN N ELSE p - 1
Right(p) == IF p = N THEN 1 ELSE p + 1

(* Action: philosopher p becomes hungry *)
Hungry(p) == states' = [states EXCEPT ![p] = 1] /\ forks' = forks

(* Action: philosopher p picks up forks if both are free *)
PickUp(p) == 
    /\ forks[Left(p)] = 0
    /\ forks[Right(p)] = 0
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![Left(p)] = p, ![Right(p)] = p]
    /\ states' = [states EXCEPT ![p] = 2]

(* Action: philosopher p puts down forks *)
PutDown(p) == 
    /\ forks[Left(p)] = p
    /\ forks[Right(p)] = p
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![Left(p)] = 0, ![Right(p)] = 0]
    /\ states' = [states EXCEPT ![p] = 0]

Next == \E p \in 1..N : 
    (states[p] = 0 /\ Hungry(p)) \/ 
    (states[p] = 1 /\ PickUp(p)) \/ 
    (states[p] = 2 /\ PutDown(p))

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<forks, states>>

---- MODULE TokenRing ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS N, M, Node

VARIABLE c

PassToken(i) == 
  /\ i /= 0
  /\ c[i] /= c[i - 1]
  /\ c' = [c EXCEPT ![i] = c[i - 1]]

CreateToken == 
  /\ c[0] = c[N - 1]
  /\ c' = [c EXCEPT ![0] = (c[N - 1] + 1) % M]

Next == 
  \/ \E i \in Node : PassToken(i)
  \/ CreateToken

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<c>> /\ WF_<<c>>(Next)

TypeOK == 
  /\ c \in [Node -> 0 .. M - 1]

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A simple clock that counts from 0 to MAX and wraps around.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE Clock ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES clock, MAX

Init == /\ clock = 0
       /\ MAX \in Nat
       /\ MAX > 0

Next == /\ clock' = IF clock = MAX THEN 0 ELSE clock + 1
       /\ MAX' = MAX

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<clock, MAX>>

---- MODULE PetersonsAlgorithm ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES flag, turn

        /\ pin = "NoPIN"

          /\ state' = "PINEntered"

          /\ pin' = pin
          /\ dispensed' = "NoDispense"
          /\ state' = "Dispensing"

       \/ /\ state = "Dispensing"

          /\ pin' = pin
          /\ dispensed' = "Dispense"
          /\ state' = "Done"

       \/ /\ state = "Done"
          /\ card' = "NoCard"
          /\ pin' = "NoPIN"
          /\ dispensed' = "NoDispense"
          /\ state' = "Idle"

(* --specification -- *)
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, card, pin, dispensed>>

## Protocol / intent
This specification formalizes Resource Allocator as a TLA+ module. From the authors: ) The state space is organized around variables unsat, alloc. SANY analysis identifies 4 action-level operators and 5 temporal-level operators. Weak fairness (WF_) on action(s). Strong fairness (SF_) on action(s).

## Algorithm (title)
Resource Allocator

## Constants / parameters
Clients, Resources

## State variables
unsat, alloc

## Init (SANY-style summary)
unsat = [{}] /\ alloc = [{}]

## Actions (names + intent)
- **Request**: A client c may request a set of resources provided that all of its *) (* previous requests have been satisfied and that it doesn't hold any *) (* resources.
  transition sketch: `unsat[c] = {} /\ alloc[c] = {} /\ S /= {} /\ unsat' = [unsat EXCEPT (Seq(c), S)] /\ UNCHANGED alloc`
- **Allocate**: Allocation of a set of available resources to a client that *) (* requested them (the entire request does not have to be filled).
  transition sketch: `S /= {} /\ S \subseteq \intersect(available, unsat[c]) /\ alloc' = [alloc EXCEPT (Seq(c), \union(alloc Seq(c), S))] /\ unsat' = [unsat EXCEPT (Seq(c), unsat Seq(c) \ S)]`
- **Return**: Client c returns a set of resources that it holds. It may do so *) (* even before its full request has been honored.
  transition sketch: `S /= {} /\ S \subseteq alloc[c] /\ alloc' = [alloc EXCEPT (Seq(c), alloc Seq(c) \ S)] /\ UNCHANGED unsat`

## Next / Spec / fairness (summary)
\Exists c, S : Request(c, S) \/ Allocate(c, S) \/ Return(c, S)

Task:
A memory allocator managing a fixed pool of N pages. Clients request and release pages. Safety: no page is allocated to two clients simultaneously.

Hints: VARIABLES free, allocated. allocated is a function from Client to SUBSET Pages.

EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS Pages, Clients

            /\ inventory' = inventory
            /\ balance \in Nat
            /\ inventory \in FiniteSet

Next == InsertCoin \/ DispenseItem \/ Reset \/ AddItem \/ RemoveItem \/ CheckBalance \/ CheckInventory \/ CheckType

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<balance, inventory>>

TypeOK == /\ balance \in Nat

---- MODULE ATM ----

(* State machine states *)

CardInserted == state = "CardInserted"
PINEntered == state = "PINEntered"
Dispensing == state = "Dispensing"
Done == state = "Done"

(* TypeOK: ensures state is one of the defined states *)

        /\ pin = ""
        /\ dispensed = ""

          /\ card' = card
          /\ pin' = ""
          /\ dispensed' = ""
          /\ state' = "PINEntered"
        \/ /\ state = "PINEntered"
          /\ card' = card
          /\ pin' = "1234"  (* Assume correct PIN is "1234" *)
          /\ dispensed' = ""
          /\ state' = "Dispensing"
        \/ /\ state = "Dispensing"
          /\ card' = card
          /\ pin' = pin
          /\ dispensed' = "CashDispensed"
          /\ state' = "Done"
        \/ /\ state = "Done"
          /\ card' = ""
          /\ pin' = ""
          /\ dispensed' = ""
          /\ state' = "Idle"

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, card, pin, dispensed>>

Hints: Nodes 1..N, token position as a variable.

---- MODULE TokenRing ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS N, M, Node

VARIABLE c

Init == c \in [Node -> 0 .. M - 1]

CreateToken == /\ c[0] = c[N - 1]
              /\ c' = [c EXCEPT ![0] = (c[N - 1] + 1) % M]

PassToken(i) == /\ i /= 0
               /\ c[i] /= c[i - 1]
               /\ c' = [c EXCEPT ![i] = c[i - 1]]

Next == CreateToken \/ \E i \in Node : PassToken(i)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<c>> /\ WF_<<c>>(Next)

  Dispensing,

(* --actions
action
  InsertCard(card) == /\ state = Idle
                    /\ card \in CARD

                    /\ card' = card

  EnterPIN(pin) == /\ state = CardInserted
                   /\ pin \in PIN
                   /\ state' = PINEntered
                   /\ card' = card
                   /\ pin' = pin
                   /\ dispensed' = NULL
  Dispense(amount) == /\ state = PINEntered
                     /\ amount \in AMOUNT
                     /\ state' = Dispensing
                     /\ card' = card
                     /\ pin' = pin
                     /\ dispensed' = amount
  Done == /\ state = Dispensing
           /\ state' = Done
           /\ card' = card
           /\ pin' = pin
           /\ dispensed' = NULL
  Reset == /\ state = Done
            /\ state' = Idle
            /\ card' = NULL
            /\ pin' = NULL
            /\ dispensed' = NULL
*)

  PIN == 1..10
  AMOUNT == 1..10

  IsAmount(amount) == amount \in AMOUNT

  IsIdle(state) == state = Idle
  IsCardInserted(state) == state = CardInserted
  IsPINEntered(state) == state = PINEntered
  IsDispensing(state) == state = Dispensing
  IsDone(state) == state = Done
*)

  IsEnterPIN(state, pin) == /\ state = CardInserted
                            /\ pin \in PIN
  IsDispense(state, amount) == /\ state = PINEntered
                               /\ amount \in AMOUNT
  IsDone(state) == /\ state = Dispensing
  IsReset(state) == /\ state = Done
*)

(* --action definitions
action
  InsertCard(card) == /\ state = Idle
                    /\ card \in CARD
                    /\ state' = CardInserted
                    /\ card' = card
                    /\ pin' = NULL
                    /\ dispensed' = NULL
  EnterPIN(pin) == /\ state = CardInserted
                   /\ pin \in PIN
                   /\ state' = PINEntered
                   /\ card' = card
                   /\ pin' = pin
                   /\ dispensed' = NULL
  Dispense(amount) == /\ state = PINEntered
                     /\ amount \in AMOUNT

                     /\ card' = card
                     /\ pin' = pin
                     /\ dispensed' = amount
  Done == /\ state = Dispensing
           /\ state' = Done
           /\ card' = card
           /\ pin' = pin
           /\ dispensed' = NULL
  Reset == /\ state = Done
            /\ state' = Idle
            /\ card' = NULL
            /\ pin' = NULL
            /\ dispensed' = NULL
*)

spec

          /\ card = NULL
          /\ pin = NULL
          /\ dispensed = NULL
  Next == \/ InsertCard(card)

         \/ Dispense(amount)

  Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, card, pin, dispensed>>
*)

(* --type invariant
type
  TypeOK == /\ state \in <<Idle, CardInserted, PINEntered, Dispensing, Done>>

---- MODULE TokenRing ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS N, M, Node

VARIABLE c

Init == /\ c \in [Node -> 0 .. M - 1]

CreateToken == /\ c[0] = c[N - 1]
              /\ c' = [c EXCEPT ![0] = (c[N - 1] + 1) % M]

PassToken(i) == /\ i /= 0
               /\ c[i] /= c[i - 1]
               /\ c' = [c EXCEPT ![i] = c[i - 1]]

Next == CreateToken \/ \E i \in Node : PassToken(i)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<c>> /\ WF_<<c>>(Next)

TypeOK == /\ c \in [Node -> 0 .. M - 1]
          /\ N \in Nat
          /\ M \in Nat
          /\ M > 0
          /\ N > 0

Task:
A token ring network with N nodes. Each node passes a single token clockwise. Only the token holder may send a message.

Hints: Nodes 1..N, token position as a variable.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE TokenRing ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS N, M, Node

VARIABLE c

Init == 
  /\ c \in [Node -> 0 .. M - 1]

CreateToken == 
  /\ c[0] = c[N - 1]
  /\ c' = [c EXCEPT ![0] = (c[N - 1] + 1) % M]

PassToken(i) == 
  /\ i /= 0
  /\ c[i] /= c[i - 1]
  /\ c' = [c EXCEPT ![i] = c[i - 1]]

Next == 
  \/ CreateToken
  \/ \E i \in Node : PassToken(i)

Spec == 
  Init /\ [][Next]_<<c>> /\ WF_<<c>>(Next)

TypeOK == 
  /\ c \in [Node -> 0 .. M - 1]

A traffic light controller that cycles through Red, Green, Yellow states.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE TrafficLight ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES state, timer

Red == 0
Green == 1
Yellow == 2

        /\ timer = 0

Next == \/ /\ state = Red
           /\ timer' = timer + 1
           /\ timer' <= 10
           /\ state' = state
        \/ /\ state = Green
           /\ timer' = timer + 1
           /\ timer' <= 10
           /\ state' = state
        \/ /\ state = Yellow
           /\ timer' = timer + 1
           /\ timer' <= 10
           /\ state' = state
        \/ /\ state = Red
           /\ timer' = 0
           /\ state' = Green
        \/ /\ state = Green
           /\ timer' = 0
           /\ state' = Yellow
        \/ /\ state = Yellow
           /\ timer' = 0
           /\ state' = Red

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, timer>>

A distributed lock service where N nodes can request, acquire, and release a lock. At most one node holds the lock at any time.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE DistLock ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES lock, queue

Init == /\ lock = 0
        /\ queue = <<>>

Next == \/ /\ lock = 0
            /\ queue' = Append(queue, 1)
            /\ lock' = 1
        \/ /\ lock > 0
            /\ queue' = Tail(queue)
            /\ lock' = 0

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<lock, queue>>

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A readers-writers lock where multiple readers can hold the lock simultaneously but writers are exclusive.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE ReadersWriters ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES readers, writers, lock

Init == /\ readers = <<>>
       /\ writers = <<>>
       /\ lock = 0

Next == /\ \E r \in readers : /\ r \in readers
                            /\ ~(r \in writers)
                            /\ lock = 1
       \/ \E w \in writers : /\ w \in writers
                            /\ ~(w \in readers)
                            /\ lock = 0

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<readers, writers, lock>>

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A producer-consumer system with a bounded buffer. Multiple producers and one consumer. Buffer must never overflow or underflow.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE MultiProducerConsumer ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES buffer, bufferSize, producers, consumer

Init == /\ bufferSize \in Nat
        /\ bufferSize > 0
        /\ buffer \in Seq(1..bufferSize)
        /\ buffer = <<>>
        /\ producers \in Nat
        /\ producers > 0
        /\ consumer \in Nat
        /\ consumer > 0

ProducerAction == /\ bufferSize > Len(buffer)
                 /\ buffer' = Append(buffer, 1)

ConsumerAction == /\ Len(buffer) > 0
                 /\ buffer' = Tail(buffer)

Next == /\ bufferSize \in Nat
       /\ bufferSize > 0
       /\ buffer \in Seq(1..bufferSize)
       /\ buffer' \in Seq(1..bufferSize)
       /\ buffer' = IF Len(buffer) < bufferSize THEN Append(buffer, 1) ELSE Tail(buffer)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<buffer, bufferSize, producers, consumer>>

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

An elevator controller for a building with N floors. The elevator moves up and down, opening doors at requested floors.
I'll ensure the TLA+ syntax is correct and the core logic is sound.

---- MODULE Elevator ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES
  currentFloor, direction, requests, doorsOpen, moving, nextFloor

(* Constants *)
N == 10

(* Helper definitions *)
Up == 1
Down == -1
Idle == 0

(* Type invariants *)
TypeOK == /\ currentFloor \in 1..N
          /\ direction \in {Up, Down, Idle}
          /\ requests \in SUBSET 1..N
          /\ doorsOpen \in BOOLEAN
          /\ moving \in BOOLEAN
          /\ nextFloor \in 1..N

(* Initial state *)
Init == /\ currentFloor = 1
        /\ direction = Idle
        /\ requests = {}
        /\ doorsOpen = FALSE
        /\ moving = FALSE
        /\ nextFloor = 1

(* Next-state relation *)
Next == 
  (* Request handling *)
  \/ /\ requests' = requests \cup {f \in 1..N : f \in requests}
      /\ doorsOpen' = doorsOpen
      /\ moving' = moving
      /\ direction' = direction
      /\ nextFloor' = nextFloor
  \/ (* Doors open at requested floor *)
      /\ doorsOpen = FALSE
      /\ requests' = requests \ {currentFloor}
      /\ doorsOpen' = TRUE
      /\ moving' = FALSE
      /\ direction' = Idle
      /\ nextFloor' = currentFloor
  \/ (* Doors close after being open *)
      /\ doorsOpen = TRUE
      /\ doorsOpen' = FALSE
      /\ moving' = FALSE
      /\ direction' = Idle
      /\ nextFloor' = currentFloor
  \/ (* Elevator moves to next floor *)
      /\ doorsOpen = FALSE
      /\ moving = TRUE
      /\ nextFloor' = 
          IF currentFloor < N THEN currentFloor + 1 ELSE currentFloor
      /\ currentFloor' = nextFloor'
      /\ direction' = 
          IF currentFloor' = N THEN Down ELSE direction
      /\ moving' = TRUE
      /\ doorsOpen' = FALSE
      /\ requests' = requests

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<currentFloor, direction, requests, doorsOpen, moving, nextFloor>>

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A readers-writers lock where multiple readers can hold the lock simultaneously but writers are exclusive.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE ReadersWriters ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES readers, writers, waitingWriters

Init == /\ readers = 0
        /\ writers = 0
        /\ waitingWriters = 0

Next == \/ /\ readers > 0
          /\ readers' = readers - 1
          /\ writers' = writers
          /\ waitingWriters' = waitingWriters
        \/ /\ readers = 0
          /\ writers = 0
          /\ waitingWriters > 0
          /\ readers' = 0
          /\ writers' = 1
          /\ waitingWriters' = waitingWriters - 1
        \/ /\ readers = 0
          /\ writers = 0
          /\ waitingWriters = 0
          /\ readers' = readers + 1
          /\ writers' = writers
          /\ waitingWriters' = waitingWriters

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<readers, writers, waitingWriters>>

---- MODULE ResourceAlloc ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES owner, pending

        /\ pending = <<>>

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

Peterson's mutual exclusion algorithm for exactly 2 processes.

Hints: VARIABLES flag[2], turn.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE PetersonsAlgorithm ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES flag, turn

Init == /\ flag = [i \in 1..2 |-> FALSE]
        /\ turn = 1

Next == \/ /\ flag[1] = FALSE
          /\ flag[2] = FALSE
          /\ turn = 1
        \/ /\ flag[1] = TRUE
          /\ turn = 1
          /\ flag[2] = FALSE
          /\ turn = 2
        \/ /\ flag[1] = FALSE
          /\ flag[2] = TRUE
          /\ turn = 1
        \/ /\ flag[1] = TRUE
          /\ turn = 2
          /\ flag[2] = TRUE
          /\ turn = 1
        \/ /\ flag[1] = FALSE
          /\ flag[2] = TRUE
          /\ turn = 2
          /\ flag[1] = TRUE
          /\ turn = 2
          /\ flag[2] = FALSE
          /\ turn = 1
          /\ flag[1] = FALSE
          /\ flag[2] = FALSE
          /\ turn = 1

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<flag, turn>>

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A bank account with deposit and withdraw operations. Balance must never go negative.
I'll ensure the TLA+ syntax is correct and the core logic is sound.

---- MODULE BankAccount ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES balance, pendingDeposit, pendingWithdraw

Init == /\ balance = 0
        /\ pendingDeposit = 0
        /\ pendingWithdraw = 0

Deposit == /\ pendingDeposit' = pendingDeposit + 1
          /\ pendingWithdraw' = pendingWithdraw
          /\ balance' = balance

Withdraw == /\ pendingDeposit' = pendingDeposit
          /\ pendingWithdraw' = pendingWithdraw + 1
          /\ balance' = balance

Next == Deposit \/ Withdraw

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<balance, pendingDeposit, pendingWithdraw>>

TypeOK == /\ balance \in Int
         /\ pendingDeposit \in Nat
         /\ pendingWithdraw \in Nat
         /\ balance >= 0

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A readers-writers lock where multiple readers can hold the lock simultaneously but writers are exclusive.
I'll write a well-formed TLA+ specification with proper Init, Next, and invariants.

---- MODULE ReadersWriters ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES readers, writers, waitingWriters, waitingReaders, lock

Init == /\ readers = 0
        /\ writers = 0
        /\ waitingWriters = 0
        /\ waitingReaders = 0
        /\ lock = 0

Next == \/ /\ lock = 0
           /\ waitingReaders > 0
           /\ readers' = readers + 1
           /\ waitingReaders' = waitingReaders - 1
           /\ lock' = 1
           /\ writers' = writers
           /\ waitingWriters' = waitingWriters
       \/ /\ lock = 0
           /\ waitingWriters > 0
           /\ writers' = writers + 1
           /\ waitingWriters' = waitingWriters - 1
           /\ lock' = 1
           /\ readers' = readers
           /\ waitingReaders' = waitingReaders
       \/ /\ lock = 1
           /\ readers > 0
           /\ readers' = readers - 1
           /\ lock' = IF readers' = 0 THEN 0 ELSE 1
           /\ writers' = writers
           /\ waitingReaders' = waitingReaders
           /\ waitingWriters' = waitingWriters
       \/ /\ lock = 1
           /\ writers > 0
           /\ writers' = writers - 1
           /\ lock' = 0
           /\ readers' = readers
           /\ waitingReaders' = waitingReaders
           /\ waitingWriters' = waitingWriters

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<readers, writers, waitingWriters, waitingReaders, lock>>

VARIABLES state, card, pin, dispensed


(* BEGIN
  state == "Idle";
  card == "None";
  pin == "None";
  dispensed == 0;

  (* --state Idle --*)
  state == "Idle" => 
    (* --action InsertCard --*)
    card' == "Inserted" /\ pin' == "None" /\ dispensed' == 0 /\ state' == "CardInserted";
  (* --state CardInserted --*)
  state == "CardInserted" => 
    (* --action EnterPIN --*)
    pin' == "Entered" /\ dispensed' == 0 /\ state' == "PINEntered";
  (* --state PINEntered --*)
  state == "PINEntered" => 
    (* --action DispenseCash --*)
    dispensed' == 100 /\ state' == "Dispensing";
  (* --state Dispensing --*)
  state == "Dispensing" => 
    (* --action Finish --*)
    dispensed' == 0 /\ state' == "Done";
  (* --state Done --*)
  state == "Done" => 
    (* --action Reset --*)
    card' == "None" /\ pin' == "None" /\ dispensed' == 0 /\ state' == "Idle";
  (* --state --*)
  state' == state /\ card' == card /\ pin' == pin /\ dispensed' == dispensed;
END *)

(* --state machine --*)
Init == 
  /\ state = "Idle"
  /\ card = "None"
  /\ pin = "None"
  /\ dispensed = 0

Next == 
  (* --action InsertCard --*)
  /\ state' = "CardInserted"
  /\ card' = "Inserted"
  /\ pin' = "None"
  /\ dispensed' = 0
  /\ state = "Idle"

  \/ (* --action EnterPIN --*)
  /\ state' = "PINEntered"
  /\ card' = card
  /\ pin' = "Entered"
  /\ dispensed' = 0
  /\ state = "CardInserted"

  \/ (* --action DispenseCash --*)
  /\ state' = "Dispensing"
  /\ card' = card
  /\ pin' = pin
  /\ dispensed' = 100
  /\ state = "PINEntered"

  \/ (* --action Finish --*)
  /\ state' = "Done"
  /\ card' = card
  /\ pin' = pin
  /\ dispensed' = 0
  /\ state = "Dispensing"

  \/ (* --action Reset --*)
  /\ state' = "Idle"
  /\ card' = "None"
  /\ pin' = "None"
  /\ dispensed' = 0
  /\ state = "Done"

  \/ (* --action NoChange --*)
  /\ state' = state
  /\ card' = card
  /\ pin' = pin
  /\ dispensed' = dispensed

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, card, pin, dispensed>>

Init ==

  /\ votes = [p \in participants |-> "unknown"]
  /\ decision = "unknown"

Next ==
  \/ /\ decision = "unknown"
     /\ \E p \in participants :
        /\ votes[p] = "unknown"
        /\ votes' = [votes EXCEPT ![p] = "yes"]
        /\ decision' = decision
  \/ /\ decision = "unknown"
     /\ \E p \in participants :
        /\ votes[p] = "unknown"
        /\ votes' = [votes EXCEPT ![p] = "no"]
        /\ decision' = decision
  \/ /\ decision = "unknown"
     /\ \A p \in participants : votes[p] = "yes"
     /\ decision' = "commit"
     /\ votes' = votes
  \/ /\ decision = "unknown"
     /\ \E p \in participants : votes[p] = "no"
     /\ decision' = "abort"
     /\ votes' = votes
  \/ /\ decision \in {"commit", "abort"}
     /\ votes' = votes
     /\ decision' = decision

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<participants, votes, decision>>

   - Never let a variable grow without bound.

A counter that starts at 0, can increment by 1, and has a maximum value MAX.

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<counter>>

          /\ counter <= MAX
